gaps.description: The BoR at version 1.0.0-seed has all six articles marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. The MSP states it is 'strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights' and any conflicting MSP rule is 'void on its face.' Without defined scope, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservations, amendment protocol, or interpretation rules, Arbiter (#190) cannot meaningfully enforce the scope gate at AP adjudication. The DIO operates without constitutional boundaries, making the entire two-gate Nomos architecture rely on an empty first gate.

The MSP bootstrap seed explicitly contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules via the PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout flow. Nomos (#200) has no operational policy to evaluate at the second compliance gate, meaning Action Proposals are only gated by the BoR scope check — which itself is scaffold-only (GAP-001). The DIO's operational constitution is effectively empty.

The MSP references genome documents that 'extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.' At seed, none exist. With 24+ organs registered and actively cycling in Spine — including probabilistic services (Minder, Radiant, Soul, Glia, Lobe) that carry inherently higher operational risk — there are no organ-specific operational boundaries, risk-tier constraints, or operating rules. Every organ operates without codified guardrails.

Spine state transitions show all 24+ organs simultaneously missing pong heartbeats and transitioning to DEGRADED, then recovering via pong_recovered approximately 30 seconds later. This pattern repeats continuously. The governance triad — Senate (#220, MSP amendment authority), Nomos (#200, AP adjudication and PEM issuance), and Arbiter (#190, BoR scope verification) — are all affected. The MSP amendment pipeline requires all three: Nomos issues PEMs, Senate-Legislator drafts amendments, Arbiter verifies scope. Intermittent unavailability of all three simultaneously means the amendment pipeline cannot function reliably.

Radiant's dream cycle (Phase 1 context freshness triage + Phase 2 memory consolidation) is disabled and has never run in the current configuration. The last visible dream cycle context entries are from April 11-12, 2026 — approximately three weeks prior to the world state snapshot date of May 3, 2026. Without dream cycles, context blocks accumulate without triage, promotion, or pruning; memory entries are not consolidated; duplicate entries proliferate. This degrades the DIO's operational state coherence, which underpins Nomos's ability to evaluate Action Proposals against current world state.

SafeVault backups show a recurring pattern of failures: NAS mount denied (April 27, 28, May 1, 3 — 'Operation not permitted'), zombie processes blocking new backups (May 3 — two ~25h zombie processes from May 2 still alive and blocking sqlite3 .backup on spine.db), and script bugs causing early termination with no upload (April 30 — ORGAN_SQLITE_DBS array unbound). The MSP amendment protocol depends on Graph adapter hash verification and append-only msp_version concepts — which require durable storage. Without reliable backups, the amendment audit trail is at risk.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-001",
      "title": "Undefined Bill of Rights – constitutional vacuum",
      "description": "The BoR scaffold awaits human authorship; all six articles (Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, Interpretation Rules) are undefined. The DIO has no constitutional boundaries, making its enterprise scope unbounded and actions permissive without human-defined prohibitions or identity.",
      "grounding": "BoR Articles I–VI (all undefined); MSP 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights' subordinates MSP to BoR, but BoR missing.",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority_score": 125
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-002",
      "title": "MSP lacks substantive rules, objectives, and OKRs",
      "description": "MSP version 1.0.0-seed contains no rules ('The bootstrap seed contains no rules'). No strategic objectives, priority programs, longer-arc goals, or OKRs are defined. Nomos evaluates Action Proposals under default-permissive mode, providing no operational guidance or strategic alignment. The Senate has not published any rules via the PEM→Legislator→review→Rollout pipeline.",
      "grounding": "MSP § Rules (empty); MSP § Purpose (translates BoR into machine-actionable objectives – but BoR absent, no translation possible); MSP § Genome References (none).",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority_score": 100
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-003",
      "title": "Dream cycle scheduling disabled – memory consolidation health absent from MSP strategic objectives",
      "description": "The radiant memory system's dream cycle is 'scheduled: disabled', 'health: never_run', and last_dream is null. Autonomous memory consolidation (Phase1 triage and Phase2 LLM merging) is not running, risking context/memory bloat, fragmentation, and loss of reliable institutional knowledge. This undermines the DIO's ability to make informed decisions at Nomos compliance gates. The MSP contains no rule or strategic objective requiring memory health or dream cycle operation.",
      "grounding": "MSP § Purpose and § Rules lack any requirement for memory lifecycle management; BoR Article III (Core Identity) missing, which could define identity-preserving memory consistency obligations.",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority_score": 48
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "BOR_REGISTRATION_MISSING",
      "description": "No bor_version concept registered in Graph for the current Bill of Rights scaffold. Arbiter (#190) is required to verify the BoR by hash at every scope query. With governance_state empty, Arbiter will reject the BoR, causing all Action Proposals to fail the scope gate.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "BoR Hash Registration note: 'When this document is modified, the new SHA-256 hash must be registered in Graph as a bor_version concept before Arbiter will accept it.'",
        "MSP Arbiter Reference: 'Arbiter (#190) verifies the Bill of Rights at every scope query; it does NOT verify the MSP.'"
      ],
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "Complete denial of all AP authorization. The DIO cannot execute any actions because the scope gate cannot be passed.",
      "why_compliance_attention_needed": "The BoR scaffold exists as a document but has never been registered in the governance state, making the DIO effectively inoperative under its own rules and violating the MSP's reliance on a verifiable BoR.",
      "priority": "CRITICAL"
    },
    {
      "id": "BOR_ARTICLE_I_UNDEFINED",
      "description": "Article I – Scope of Enterprise is marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. No enterprise perimeter exists.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "BoR Article I: Scope of Enterprise",
        "MSP Purpose: 'The MSP translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives...' and 'Arbiter scope gate (BoR perimeter)'"
      ],
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "Without a defined scope, the scope gate is vacuous; any action could be considered within enterprise boundaries. This undermines the entire adjudication framework and exposes the DIO to risk of unbounded operation.",
      "why_compliance_attention_needed": "The human principal must define the legitimate operating domain of the DIO. Currently no constraints can be applied, making the BoR unenforceable.",
      "priority": "CRITICAL"
    },
    {
      "id": "BOR_ARTICLE_II_UNDEFINED",
      "description": "Article II – Prohibited Activities is undefined. No actions are explicitly forbidden.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "BoR Article II: Prohibited Activities",
        "MSP Relationship to the Bill of Rights: 'Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face.'"
      ],
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "The DIO has no absolute prohibitions, only MSP-level constraints (which are currently empty). This leaves no safeguard against harmful or unethical actions that might otherwise be allowed by permissive MSP evaluation.",
      "why_compliance_attention_needed": "The human principal must declare the fundamental limits on DIO behavior. Without this, the BoR fails its protective purpose and the DIO could engage in activities the principal would never authorize.",
      "priority": "CRITICAL"
    },
    {
      "id": "BOR_ARTICLE_IV_UNDEFINED",
      "description": "Article IV – Human Authority Reservation is undefined. The powers reserved exclusively to humans are not specified.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "BoR Article IV: Human Authority Reservation",
        "MSP Senate-Legislator Authority note: 'Scope expansion requires a BoR amendment, which is human-only.' (implies reservation but not defined)"
      ],
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "Without clear delineation, the DIO might autonomously take decisions reserved for human judgment (e.g., changing enterprise scope, altering core identity, or making high-risk commitments), leading to governance violations and loss of human control.",
      "why_compliance_attention_needed": "The MSP references a human-only BoR amendment process, but the BoR itself does not codify what authority is reserved. This ambiguity could be exploited or misinterpreted by downstream organs.",
      "priority": "HIGH"
    },
    {
      "id": "BOR_ARTICLE_V_UNDEFINED",
      "description": "Article V – Amendment Protocol for the BoR is undefined. The process for the human principal to modify the BoR is missing.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "BoR Article V: Amendment Protocol",
        "BoR Authority: 'Human principal only. No automated system may amend this document.'",
        "BoR Hash Registration: 'When this document is modified...' (requires a defined process to be actionable)."
      ],
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "All the other gaps depend on the ability to amend the BoR. Without an amendment protocol, the human principal cannot formalize changes, the hash registration flow cannot be triggered, and the BoR remains permanently frozen in scaffold state.",
      "why_compliance_attention_needed": "The governance class state shows no events; even if the principal wanted to define the articles, there is no defined mechanism to do so, blocking all BoR maturation.",
      "priority": "CRITICAL"
    },
    {
      "id": "BOR_ARTICLE_III_UNDEFINED",
      "description": "Article III – Core Identity is undefined. The DIO's fundamental purpose, values, and identity are not stated.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "BoR Article III: Core Identity"
      ],
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "The DIO lacks alignment anchors; decisions influenced by identity-related trade-offs cannot be guided by a declared mission or ethical stand. Over time this can lead to drift from intended character and purpose.",
      "why_compliance_attention_needed": "While less blocking than scope or prohibitions, an undefined identity increases long-term risk of misalignment and makes it impossible for Nomos to apply identity-based MSP rules (none exist now, but expected).",
      "priority": "HIGH"
    },
    {
      "id": "BOR_ARTICLE_VI_UNDEFINED",
      "description": "Article VI – Interpretation Rules are undefined. No framework for resolving BoR ambiguities or conflicts exists.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "BoR Article VI: Interpretation Rules"
      ],
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "low",
      "impact": "Once other articles are defined, ambiguities will inevitably arise. Without interpretation rules, disputes cannot be formally resolved, undermining the BoR's finality and enforceability.",
      "why_compliance_attention_needed": "Although not immediately blocking, a missing interpretation clause will become a critical gap as soon as the BoR is populated. It should be addressed early in the maturation process.",
      "priority": "MEDIUM"
    }
  ]
}

    },
